title: BD FACSAria Fusion - Bi-weekly Maintenance and Cleaning Protocol
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"1kab7","text":"This Standard Operation Procedure is intended to guide the facility staff to perform scheduled maintenance and cleaning of the instrument BD FACSAria™ Fusion.","type":"align-justify","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
guidelines: {"blocks":[{"key":"d6vlu","text":"This protocol should be followed:","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"27fms","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"f00k9","text":"Preventively every 15 days.","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"90n56","text":"After running potentially bacterial loaded samples.","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"2i9qc","text":"When a bacterial/fungal contamination is detected in the weekly assessment.","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"u37b","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"feo5q","text":"Perform the steps subsequently unless otherwise stated.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
before_start: {"blocks":[{"key":"ka4u","text":"Be sure to wear gloves and lab coat at all times! ","type":"align-left","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":50}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"1spil","text":"General rule: spray 70% ethanol in all connectors before plugging any tubing!","type":"align-left","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":76}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
warning: {"blocks":[{"key":"dq360","text":"Handle FACSClean carefully. It should't enter in contact with the skin and eyes (wear gloves, lab coat and glasses) and it ruins colorful clothes.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":27}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}

Q-tips.","type":"ordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"ddcvo","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
steps: Switch on the PC, the instrument and then the FACSDiva software.
Open the fluidics cart, unplug the waste tank, discard it's contents and reconnect it.
In the software, go to “Cytometer” → “Fluidics Startup”
Check if the closed loop nozzle is installed and Click “Done” for the two first options.
Once the first Startup with FACSClean is done, wait for 00:15:00 , then repeat step 6. go to step #6

Remove the cleaning tank from the fluidics cart. Replace the FACSClean Becton Dickinson (BD) Catalog #340345 in the cleaning tank with at least 2 L of distilled (DI) H 2 O. Re-install it into the fluidics cart.
Repeat step 6. go to step #6
After the previous step is done, remove the closed loop nozzle and put into the 50 mL tube containing DI H 2 O.
Remove the nozzle lock, put into the Clean Compartment box and spray Incidin Foam ECOLAB Catalog #3046010 over it.
Soak Q-tips with Incidin Foam ECOLAB Catalog #3046010 and insert them into the nozzle slot and nozzle lock slot. Wait 00:10:00 .
Clean all the parts in the box with 70% Ethanol and re-install them.

Remove the bypass and install an autoclaved sheath filter (make sure to install it in the correct direction of the flow). Don't forget to close the ventilation valves in the filter, otherwise sheath fluid will be flooding the fluidics cart!
Remove the Q-tips, clean and dry both the closed loop nozzle and the nozzle lock and re-install them. Repeat step 6 (2x if using 130 µM nozzle next). go to step #6

Remove the cleaning tank from the fluidics cart. Put the newly autoclaved and fully filled sheath tank in the fluidics cart. Connect the fluidics and air connections to the sheath tank. Connect the sheath tank sensor.
Grease the O-ring of the sample pressure chamber (by greasing the socket of the tube holder).
In the software go to “Cytometer” → “Cleaning modes” → “Sample line backflush”. Open the side filter valves under an empty bottle to remove trapped air (500 mL).
Prepare the sheath tank and filter for autoclaving.

Disconnect the grey plastic CPC quick connector from the ethanol filter and plug it's correspondent connector in the bypass. Disconnect the air supply from the ethanol tank and plug it to the cleaning tank.

Fill the cleaning tank with 5 L of FACSClean Becton Dickinson (BD) Catalog #340345 and place it into the fluidics cart. Plug the metal CPC coupling to the cleaning tank. Wear protective clothes, gloves and glasses every time FACSClean is handled in this protocol.
Install the “Bypass” as a placeholder for the sheath filter.
Rinse the sheath filter with deionized H 2 O on the GeneCore faucet (This step can be done in between the next steps).
Unplug the metal connectors in the filter on both sides. Over the sink, open the ventilation valves and let the inside liquid to flow out.
Disconnect the sheath tank sensor and remove the sheath tank from the fluidics cart.
Unplug the fluidics connection from the sheath tank.
In the meantime of the FACSClean startup cleaning, remove the parts for cleaning and put them in the Cleaning Compartment box.
Spray Incidin Foam on the following parts part: (Deflection plates, tube holder, splash shield, nozzle holder).

Spray carefully the entire sort chamber (use first MAXX Windus C2 ECOLAB Catalog #9084640 to remove the salt crystals, then after some minutes follow with 70% Ethanol) and dry it with soft tissue paper.
Clean the keyboard and biosafety cabinet with Incidin Foam ECOLAB Catalog #3046010 .
Remove the cleaning tank from the fluidics cart. Replace the DI H 2 O in the cleaning tank with at least 2 L 70% Ethanol. Re-install it into the fluidics cart.
Repeat step 6. go to step #6
Insert the desired nozzle and start the stream.
creator: Daniel Gimenes
